prompt:
Use case: identity-preserve
Asset type: runtime tactics unit sprite
Primary request: preserve the exact approved Aevum Guardian look from this source image, but make the bare chest visible like the source concept and change the weapon to a staff
Subject: the exact same Children of the Prophecy Aevum Guardian with the same broad conical hat, wrapped face, lean ascetic proportions, rust-red sash, trousers, pose, and pixelation level, but with the chest clearly visible and the weapon reading as a staff instead of a spear
Style/medium: exactly the same painterly pixel-art-inspired tactics sprite finish and moderate pixelation level as the input image
Composition/framing: preserve the same full-body pose, crop, orientation, silhouette, and transparent background as closely as possible
Lighting/mood: preserve the same dust-filtered dusk lighting and restrained contrast from the input image
Color palette: preserve the same bone white, ash beige, muted rust red, brown leather, and tiny teal accent palette from the input image
Constraints: change only what is necessary to expose the bare chest and convert the weapon from spear to staff; preserve the character identity, hat, face wrap, pose, trousers, sash, anatomy, and rendering finish; the weapon must read clearly as a simple staff or ritual staff with no spear tip, no blade, and no pointed lance silhouette; keep the full character body and staff fully opaque with alpha only outside the outer silhouette; exactly one subject; no floor; no scenery; no text; no extra subjects; no oversized empty padding
Avoid: costume redesign, heavier robes, chest fully covered, spear tip, blade, polearm head, glossy rendering, soft blur, anime rendering, cinematic background
